changelog:
WebSocket transport for /v1/responses (Codex CLI experimental).

When Codex CLI sends a WebSocket upgrade on /v1/responses (or /backend-api/codex/responses) with the experimental responses_websockets feature flag, the proxy now upgrades the connection and streams the response as Codex-shaped JSON envelopes ({event, data}) over a single text-message channel instead of HTTP SSE. HTTP SSE remains the default — both transports share the same upstream pipeline (ClaudeToKiro -> Kiro -> response synthesis).

Client setup (~/.codex/config.toml):
  [model_providers.kiro-go]
  name = "OpenAI"
  base_url = "http://your-host:8989/backend-api/codex"
  wire_api = "responses"
  supports_websockets = true
  requires_openai_auth = true
  [features]
  responses_websockets = true

New route alias: /backend-api/codex/responses (matches codex-lb's path so existing Codex configs work unchanged).

The gorilla/websocket dependency was added (v1.5.3). HTTP fallback is automatic if the upgrade fails or the client doesn't request it.

A10: dashboard API Keys tab + Analytics tab.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
A5: drop Claude Code system prompt entirely when detected.
A4: drop ThinkingModePrompt; split filter chain.
A3: drop --- SYSTEM PROMPT --- markers; structured tool results only.
A2: kiro-go-patch container_name + pull_policy: always.
A1: <system-reminder>/x-anthropic-billing-header strip; /v1/responses; model id echo.
